research_question: Can smaller local models and diverse hosted model routes execute complex bounded implementation tasks when a frontier model removes ambiguity before execution and deterministic code controls the runtime loop?
thesis: Use frontier capability to author and freeze the specification, interfaces, invariants, tests, and cards; use deterministic code to dispatch and gate; place smaller models inside bounded implementation cells.

card_authoring.attribution_source: Codex session metadata and the contemporary handoff
card_authoring.manifest_metadata_error: The frozen manifest says author_effort high; session turn metadata consistently records GPT-5.5 medium.

runtime_controller.implementation: plain shell and Python state machine
fixed_responsibilities: iterate through the hard-coded ordered card array and skip later cards after a terminal failure
run the v1 functional, anti-stub, test-integrity, and visible top-level directory-scope checks
append one evidence row per event within the current sweep invocation
continue or stop from explicit verdicts
known_v1_limits: working-directory and prompt isolation, not an operating-system sandbox
scope observation did not cover dotfiles, off-tree writes, reads, every deletion, or every copied context-file modification
the output file was truncated at sweep start and was append-only only within one invocation

architecture.worker_role: Implement one materialized card and choose code/tool actions; the controller did not delegate card order, retry count, gate verdict, or its next transition to worker output.

benchmark.exclusion_reason: Card 10 contains contradictory slash semantics between its written contract, a fixed example, and its property oracle, plus an incompatible escaped-backslash assertion. The reference gate was passed with a slash hard-code and broadened backslash handling.
benchmark.prompt_guidance_disclosure: Cards 7 and 9 include prior failure evidence; Card 9 also supplies an explicit DFS/memoization implementation strategy. The recorded comparison is guided execution, not zero-shot problem solving.
acceptance_gate_order: test immutability and visible top-level directory-scope check
benchmark.retry_policy: At most one informed retry per card in the principal runner; dedicated follow-ups are preserved as separate source runs.

execution_surfaces.kind: hosted coding harness using OpenCode Go model routes

headline_results.detail: Card 9 timed out after 900 seconds with 9 of 11 tests passing, then passed 11 of 11 on an informed extended retry lasting 2,006 seconds. Post-hoc probes found failure on valid multi-letter cells, boolean acceptance, and large-integer division precision, so this is not full-contract proof.
Card 9 passed 11 of 11 tests on the first attempt in 804 seconds and passed the post-hoc valid multi-letter-cell probe, but accepted booleans and lost precision on large-integer division.

headline_results.placement: hosted through the OpenCode CLI using OpenCode Go routes

claim_status.claim: A deterministic control plane with zero LLM routing, retry, acceptance, or stop decisions can place smaller models in bounded implementation cells.
The tested local Qwen3.6 27B and Gemma 4 31B configurations made the published gates green through Card 9.
A small model can plan or execute arbitrary complex projects without frontier decomposition.
Card 10 proves a frontier-only capability boundary.
